name: Šiauliai

description:
# Šiauliai ### SQQ · v0.1.1 Part of the [EU Map Pack](https://ahkimn.github.io/subwaybuilder-eu-maps). Highly-detailed map of the Šiauliai metropolitan area using Lithuanian government statistics. ![Map Coverage](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Subway-Builder-Modded/registry/refs/heads/main/maps/yukina-lt-siauliai/gallery/preview.webp) ## Coverage

## Additional Features - **Building Collision** — A buildings index is included for all non-filtered buildings, each carrying a per-building foundation depth in in-game meters. Depths scale with building height and footprint width and are bounded to the **−10 m** to **−80 m** range: single-family homes and low-rise structures anchor at the floor, while the tallest towers with deep piles reach the cap. Freestanding towers, masts, chimneys, and other slender non-occupiable structures are detected by shape and floored to the **−10 m** minimum. Foundation depths drive underground tunnel-clearance collision and are also emitted as a visual `foundations` pmtiles layer visible on the in-game map. Building geometry comes from the GRPK national INSPIRE buildings cache; heights come from the Global Building Atlas LoD1 model with the Overture Maps Foundation footprint side-channel as a fallback (the national building registry publishes no per-building floor counts). - **Water Depth Index** — A water depth index restricts track placement in water bodies. Coastal bundles include a real bathymetric layer derived from EMODnet Bathymetry (1/16 arc-min DTM, ~115 m resolution); inland water (rivers, lakes, reservoirs) uses a flat **−5 m** index that prevents surface-level track placement. - **Ocean Foundations** — A depth-colored sea floor layer is included for coastal bundles, providing a visual representation of Baltic Sea bathymetry visible on the in-game map. - **Place Name Labels** — Three layers of place name labels are embedded in the map: - _City labels_ — Lithuanian city municipalities (_miesto savivaldybės_ — Vilnius, Kaunas, Klaipėda, Šiauliai, Panevėžys, Alytus, Palanga) at the metropolitan scale. - _Municipality labels_ — Names of all municipalities (_savivaldybės_) within the map boundary. - _Neighborhood labels_ — Sub-municipal settlement names: _seniūnaitijos_ for Vilnius and Klaipėda city subdivisions, _seniūnijos_ (wards) elsewhere, plus named settlements (_gyvenamosios vietovės_) in rural areas. ## Methodology This map is generated through a six-phase pipeline that transforms Lithuanian government census, building, and employment statistics into georeferenced origin/destination demand points. **Point Seeding:** Candidate points are placed within each settlement (_gyvenamoji vietovė_) or urban ward (_seniūnija_ / _seniūnaitija_) using population-weighted centroids derived from the native Lithuanian statistical grid — the 250 m census grid in LKS-94 (EPSG:3346) across populated areas, falling back to the 1 km grid where the 250 m cells are disclosure-suppressed — dasymetrically distributed against the GRPK national buildings cache filtered by the NtrPastatas use-class (_paskirtis_) taxonomy. **Mesh Reconciliation:** Initial point populations are refined by solving a constrained convex optimization that approximates each point's mass to residential and economic mesh ideals while preserving per-municipality (_savivaldybė_) census totals. Sub-municipal chochos cascade through two grains — named settlements in rural areas and urban wards (_seniūnijos_ in most cities, the finer _seniūnaitijos_ where a city publishes them, e.g. Vilnius and Klaipėda) within the city cores. **Aggregation:** Workplace mass is anchored to Statistics Lithuania's place-of-activity employment (the physical count of jobs located in each municipality, by economic-activity sector), so employees are placed where they actually work rather than at a registered head-office address. The financial and public-administration sectors that the municipal series omits are added from the national labour-force totals and distributed by administrative floor area. Each building's implied worker density follows the sector composition of its use-class, calibrated to the municipal employment total. **Commute Assignment:** Lithuania publishes no journey-to-work matrix, so commute flows are synthesized with a hierarchical gravity model with distance-decay, seeded by destination-keyed self-containment priors calibrated against the published commuting behaviour of peer countries (Estonia, Latvia, Czechia, Poland). The workplace-side marginal is the place-of-activity employment; the residence-side marginal is the census employed-resident count per municipality. **Special Demand Injection:** Non-commuter demand generators — airports, the seaport and ferry terminal, universities and colleges, museums, libraries, cultural centres, theatres, sports arenas, convention centres, national and regional parks, and tourism attractions — are injected as an additive layer. Airport demand from Lithuanian Airports annual passenger statistics; seaport demand from Port of Klaipėda ferry and cruise throughput; higher-education enrollment from the LAMA BPO admissions registry combined with ŠVIS institutional stock and official full-time study-form shares; museum visitor counts from the Ministry of Culture museum-activity statistics geolocated against the LIMIS museum registry; library visitors from the National Library statistics; and protected-area visitors from the State Service for Protected Areas (VSTT) monitoring. **Layout Reconciliation:** A final spatial pass resolves point overlaps and ensures map clickability while keeping special-demand locations fixed. ## Data Sources All demand modeling is derived from freely available Lithuanian government statistical datasets and public geospatial resources. ### Census & Demographics - **Native Census Grid** (Statistics Lithuania statistical grids — 250 m + 1 km national cell polygons in LKS-94 with per-cell population × sex × age, including the working-age band used as the dasymetric weight) — [Statistics Lithuania / geoportal.lt](https://www.geoportal.lt/) - **Place-of-Activity Employment** (SDMX `S8R086` — employees by physical place of activity across all 60 municipalities × NACE Rev 2 sector; the workplace-side marginal) — [Statistics Lithuania (OSP)](https://osp.stat.gov.lt/) - **Place-of-Residence Employment** (SDMX `S3R532` — employed persons by place of residence; the commute residence-side marginal) — [Statistics Lithuania (OSP)](https://osp.stat.gov.lt/) - **National Sector Control Totals** (Eurostat Labour Force Survey `lfsa_egan2` — national financial and public-administration employment, the sectors OSP does not publish at municipal grain) — [Eurostat](https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/lfs/database) ### Geospatial Reference - **Administrative Boundaries + Classifier** (INSPIRE Annex I Administrative Units — municipalities _savivaldybės_ + wards _seniūnijos_ + counties _apskritys_, with the national code classifier) — [Registrų centras / GIS-Centras](https://www.geoportal.lt/) - **Building Footprints + Use-Class** (GRPK _Georeferencinių erdvinių duomenų rinkinys_ Pastatai — national building polygons carrying the NtrPastatas use-class _paskirtis_ and the structure-type code; the sole building geometry source for both modeled demand and the building tiles) — [geoportal.lt GRPK](https://www.geoportal.lt/) - **Building Use-Class Taxonomy** (Registrų centras NtrPastatas cadastral attributes + the 91-type _NtrPaskirtiesTipas_ classifier) — [Registrų centras via data.gov.lt](https://data.gov.lt/) - **Building Height** (Global Building Atlas LoD1 heights, with the Overture Maps Foundation footprint side-channel as fallback — the national registry publishes no per-building floor counts) — [VIDA Global Building Atlas](https://vida.place/) · [Overture](https://overturemaps.org/) - **Settlement & Ward Polygons** (Registrų centras Address Register graphic model — settlements _gyvenamosios vietovės_ + wards _seniūnijos_, the sub-municipal chocho grains) — [Registrų centras via data.gov.lt](https://data.gov.lt/) - **City Sub-District Polygons** (municipal _seniūnaitija_ boundaries for Vilnius and Klaipėda city cores) — [Vilnius](https://opencity.vilnius.lt/) · [Klaipėda](https://www.klaipeda.lt/) - **Land Cover** (OpenStreetMap-normalized land use — no open fine-resolution national land-cover product exists) — [OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/) - **Bathymetry** (EMODnet Bathymetry DTM, ~115 m resolution — Baltic Sea, coastal bundles only) — [EMODnet Bathymetry Consortium](https://emodnet.ec.europa.eu/en/bathymetry) ### Special Demand Statistics - **Airport Passenger Statistics** (Lithuanian Airports annual passenger series for Vilnius, Kaunas, and Palanga) — [Lietuvos oro uostai](https://www.ltou.lt/) - **Port & Ferry Statistics** (Port of Klaipėda management report — DFDS international ferry + cruise terminal passenger throughput) — [Port of Klaipėda](https://www.portofklaipeda.lt/) - **Higher-Education Enrollment** (LAMA BPO admissions intake + ŠVIS institutional student stock + OSP full-time study-form shares, for universities and colleges) — [LAMA BPO](https://www.lamabpo.lt/) · [ŠVIS](https://www.svis.smm.lt/) - **Museums** (Ministry of Culture museum-activity statistics with per-institution annual visitor counts, geolocated against the LIMIS museum registry) — [Ministry of Culture](https://lrkm.lrv.lt/) · [LIMIS](https://www.limis.lt/) - **Libraries** (National Library of Lithuania registrar + national library-statistics with per-library physical visitor counts) — [Lietuvos nacionalinė Martyno Mažvydo biblioteka](https://www.lnb.lt/) - **Parks** (State Service for Protected Areas per-territory annual visitor monitoring for national and regional parks) — [Valstybinė saugomų teritorijų tarnyba (VSTT)](https://vstt.lrv.lt/) - **Cultural, Sports & Convention Venues** (per-venue operator statistics and reports for theatres, concert halls, arenas, stadiums, convention centres, spas, and theme parks, bounded by national culture-attendance ceilings) — [Statistics Lithuania (OSP)](https://osp.stat.gov.lt/) ## License This map data is released under the [GNU General Public License v3.0](https://github.com/ahkimn/subwaybuilder-eu-maps/blob/main/LICENSE). ## Credits Map authored by [Yukina-](https://subwaybuildermodded.com/credits/)
